rationales.hook_strength: 'You can't outrun your fork' is a known phrase but the sub reframes it with 'brutal math' — concrete, slightly threatening, earns the click.
rationales.clarity: Each slide is one mechanism with a named label and a single takeaway — no slide tries to do two things.
rationales.audience_fit: Speaks directly to people who gym but wonder why they're not leaner — exactly the tired, busy, confused 20–35 demo.
rationales.novelty: The core insight (diet matters more than exercise for fat loss) is well-worn; the tracker-overcounting angle and NEAT compensation add texture but won't feel new to anyone who follows fitness creators.
rationales.brand_fit: Plain language, hedged framing ('may burn', 'often by a lot'), no hype words, and the CTA ties directly to Vibe's food-tracking feature without being cringe.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential from the reframe slide and the 'watch lies upward' hook, but novelty ceiling will limit viral sharing compared to a truly contrarian take.
